brief.objective: explain the PRIME instructional materials correlation process
keyMessages: WIDA PRIME lists instructional materials that have gone through the PRIME process and received a PRIME seal.
PRIME 2020 includes materials aligned to the current WIDA English Language Development Standards Framework, 2020 Edition.
WIDA PRIME information helps educators consider how particular materials fit in their program.
proofPoints: The WIDA PRIME site lists reviewed PRIME v1 and v2 correlations and PRIME 2020 alignments.
The WIDA PRIME site says PRIME 2020 includes materials aligned to the current WIDA English Language Development Standards Framework, 2020 Edition.
The WIDA PRIME site says WIDA does not endorse instructional materials and PRIME is not an evaluative tool that judges effectiveness.
brief.layoutRecommendation: Use a single-column HTML email announcement with a logo header, concise message, one CTA button, and inline styles.

headlineOptions: Start your WIDA PRIME correlation process
Show how your materials align with WIDA Standards
Prepare instructional materials for WIDA PRIME
subheadOptions: WIDA PRIME lists instructional materials that have gone through the PRIME process and received a PRIME seal.
PRIME 2020 includes materials aligned to the current WIDA English Language Development Standards Framework, 2020 Edition.
Publishers can submit instructional materials and a portfolio of evidence for review by WIDA-trained reviewers.
copy.body: WIDA PRIME is a tool to assist publishers and educators in analyzing instructional materials for the presence of key components of the WIDA Standards. For publishers, the process includes reviewing the workbook and FAQs, compiling evidence, submitting materials, WIDA review, and publishing approved alignment information.
bullets: Materials may include core and supplemental texts, websites and software, and other ancillary materials.
To request the WIDA PRIME 2020 Seal, publishers submit instructional materials and a portfolio of evidence.
The WIDA PRIME site lists reviewed PRIME v1 and v2 correlations and PRIME 2020 alignments.
WIDA continues to support WIDA PRIME V2 for Spanish Language Development materials.
WIDA PRIME information helps educators consider how particular materials fit in their program.

fittedCopy.headline: Start your WIDA PRIME correlation process
fittedCopy.deck: WIDA PRIME lists instructional materials that have gone through the PRIME process and received a PRIME seal.
proofPoints: Materials may include core and supplemental texts, websites and software, and other.
To request the WIDA PRIME 2020 Seal, publishers submit instructional materials.

compositionTemplate.typographyScale: email-safe headline with concise body text
compositionTemplate.artPlateTreatment: wide hero art strip with controlled crop
compositionTemplate.copyPlacement: email body stack with 2 proof rows

designRecipe.hierarchy: compact publisher email opening: official logo header, distinctive WIDA PRIME hero strip, then app-rendered headline, deck, proof rows, and CTA
designRecipe.artDirection: wide polished email hero for WIDA PRIME, using abstract publishing-correlation imagery—stacked instructional materials, digital resource cues, alignment pathways, and subtle seal-inspired geometry—rendered in a calm professional education palette without words or logos
designRecipe.placeholderStrategy: use subject placement, controlled crop, and clean negative space to create a recognizable 680px-wide visual hook that supports the body copy below
designRecipe.appComposition: app owns the official logo header, exact headline, deck, proof rows, CTA button, email-safe layout, and export; generated art supplies only the top hero concept
promptDirectives: Design a wide 680px email hero strip, not a full newsletter page.
Make it feel like the opening visual for a publisher-facing WIDA PRIME announcement.
Use abstract instructional-materials correlation and review-process cues; keep details legible at email scale.
Do not include readable text, fake logos, seals, buttons, UI modules, contact bars, or placeholder boxes.

compositionScore.question: Would I send this to the WCEPS team without apology?
warnings: WIDA PRIME prompt is missing avoid guardrail(s): fake title.

failureModes.message: WIDA PRIME visual prompt is missing avoid guardrail(s): fake title.

renderQa.question: Would I send this rendered artifact to the WCEPS team without apology?

modelQa.question: Would a senior WCEPS reviewer send this artifact without apology?
issues: Fitted proof point appears truncated from “other ancillary materials” to “and other,” creating unfinished production copy.
warnings: Selected headline, deck, and CTA repeat PRIME/process language instead of building a more natural hierarchy.
Deck language may imply every listed material received a PRIME seal; PRIME 2020 Seal language should be distinguished from older correlations/alignments.
Image is clean and on-topic but reads as broad instructional-materials publishing rather than uniquely WIDA PRIME.

WIDA PRIME lists instructional materials that have gone through the PRIME process and received a PRIME
Materials may include core and supplemental texts, websites and software, and other.
To request the WIDA PRIME 2020 Seal, publishers submit instructional materials.

Deterministic grid, typography, headline, audience, and CTA.

imageResults.revisedPrompt:
Create a 1200x700 email-safe full-bleed campaign art plate for a polished publisher-facing WIDA PRIME announcement. Wide 680px email hero strip sensibility within the canvas: compact, premium opening visual, with the top-band as the main visual zone and a clean body/text-safe negative-space area suitable for app-rendered headline/deck/proof/CTA later. Absolutely no readable text, letters, numbers, labels, logos, wordmarks, badges, seals, buttons, UI modules, document-layout panels, fake charts, or brand-like marks.

Subject and mood: refined education-sector editorial/abstract atmosphere about K–12 instructional materials correlation, standards alignment review, Spanish language development materials, PRIME 2020 workflow, and publisher/correlator review process. Use abstract publishing-correlation imagery: stacked teacher editions and student workbooks, curriculum binders, grade-band packets, sample spreads with no readable marks, translucent alignment overlays with only non-textual lines/dots, subtle review notes with no symbols or letters, digital resource surfaces with non-readable abstract blocks, and a clean publisher review table implied by materials arrangement rather than UI.

Visual style: authentic editorial photography blended with refined abstract illustration, NY media/ad-agency quality, premium atmosphere, layered depth, intentional crop, sophisticated lighting, clean negative space, material-specific and trustworthy. Color palette inspired by #0057A8 deep blue, #00A6A6 teal, #6CC24A green, and #F6A800 amber, used as accents and translucent overlays, not logo patterns. Avoid literal brand shapes, chevrons, arrows, mountain marks, background logo patterns, fake PRIME seals, endorsement imagery, random novels, trade books, library stacks, fake book titles, publisher brands, close-up faces, detailed hands, workshop/training-room stock photography, generic meeting-table clichés, washed-out empty fades.

Composition: full artifact design comp with strong visual hierarchy and clean crop for a 680px-wide email hero. Arrange instructional-material stacks and open sample spreads diagonally across the upper/right visual band with layered depth; leave a calm, type-safe contrast zone in the lower/left or central body area for later app typography, without drawing boxes or panels. Use soft shadows, subtle paper textures, glassy non-readable alignment grids, small colored tabs with no marks, and abstract bilingual/language-development cues through color and material layering only. Minimal density, compact hierarchy, recognizable visual hook at email scale, full-bleed edges, no newsletter/page layout scaffolding.

request.goal: explain the PRIME instructional materials correlation process
request.topic: PRIME 2020, PRIME V1/V2, Spanish Language Development correlations, and publisher workflow
request.notes: Do not imply WIDA endorses materials or that PRIME evaluates effectiveness.

sourceEvidence.excerpt: WIDA PRIME is a tool to assist publishers and educators in analyzing their materials for the presence of key components of the WIDA Standards.
sourceEvidence.label: WIDA PRIME definition

sourceEvidence.excerpt: PRIME stands for Protocol for Review of Instructional Materials with the English Language Development Standards Framework, 2020 Edition.
sourceEvidence.label: PRIME 2020 meaning

sourceEvidence.excerpt: Materials may include core and supplemental texts, websites and software, and other ancillary materials.
sourceEvidence.label: Materials in scope

sourceEvidence.excerpt: WIDA continues to support WIDA PRIME V2 for Spanish Language Development materials.
sourceEvidence.label: Spanish Language Development
